items.title: Enormous ship en route to pick up world's largest electric ferry

children.content: The 217-metre-long heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin will deliver a Tasmanian shipbuilder's new electric ferry to South America, after the previous transporter became caught up in the Strait of Hormuz closure.

gallery.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean
A large electric ferry named China Zorrilla on the River Derwent in Hobart.

items.title: Premier tells member to take complaint to body which doesn't exist yet

children.content: Jeremy Rockliff won't investigate how an email containing the health-related information of a politician was leaked to the media and suggests she could take up the matter with a complaints body that doesn't exist yet.

image.altText: Man looks out to the distance, leaning on a green chair

items.title: Search continues for Tasmanian devil 'Mary' missing from Gold Coast wildlife park

children.content: The recently arrived young devil has been kept in a secure indoor facility while quarantining, but was noticed missing yesterday morning.

image.altText: Tasmanian Devil joeys walks through grass

items.title: Cost-of-living squeeze pushes Tasmanians to cut back on health and fitness

children.content: As household budgets tighten, keeping up a health and fitness regime often slips, but experts say giving people cheap or free ways to stay fit is critical to longer-term wellbeing.

gallery.altText: People working out in a community hall

thumbnail.altText: People working out in a community hall

items.title: Ban on nurses wearing colourful scrubs at Hobart hospital to be overturned

children.content: Nurses at the Royal Hobart Hospital have been told they should stop wearing fun scrubs or "free dress" and reminded they need to maintain "some professionalism" regarding their uniform.

image.altText: Woman in hospital scrubs opening a filing cabinet.

items.title: New deer hunting rules not expected to bring numbers down

children.content: The Tasmanian government is making changes to the rules for deer hunting in the state, but farmers say it won't be enough to bring numbers down, with the sector's peak body calling for the species to be declared a pest.

image.altText: Fallow deer in Tasmania Guy Ellis photo.

items.title: 'A fifth of the price': Council uses AI for 10-year strategic plan

children.content: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.

image.altText: An aerial view of a regional city with hills in the distance under an overcast, electric blue sky at sunset.

items.title: Cats eradicated from Little Dog Island

children.content: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.

image.altText: A detection dog near the beach at Little Dog Island

items.title: Antibiotic found in wild seafood 14 kilometres from salmon farm

children.content: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

image.altText: Tassal salmon pens in Macquarie Harbour, southwest Tasmania.

items.title: 'Terribly sad day': Beer experts say Boag's closure points to growing cost of brewing

children.content: Beer industry stakeholders say it's a "sad day for the history of Tassie beer" after the announcement Boag's brewery in Launceston will close.

articles.title: James Boag ends 145-year history as brewer ceases Tasmanian production

children.content: The famed northern Tasmanian brewer announces it will cease production in the state by November and repay a government loan it secured to keep its Launceston brewery visitor centre open.

image.altText: James Boags Beers 2024-06-25 10:06:00

items.title: Snowy 2.0 blows out by more than $3m a day. Will it ever be worth it?

children.content: Australia's most ambitious renewable energy project is slowly nearing completion, but experts are divided on whether Snowy 2.0 will ever justify its snowballing price tag.

image.altText: A worker waters the ground in the distance while in a tunnel and a excavator works alongside them

items.title: Trump rethinking $2.5b fund amid corruption allegations, sources say

children.content: The US president is reconsidering whether to move forward with a $2.51 billion fund for his political allies, sources say.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: Semaglutide is used for weight loss but may benefit kidneys and heart

children.content: Research shows semaglutide — commonly sold as Ozempic or Wegovy — can improve kidney function and survival rates for people with some chronic diseases, even if they have heart issues.

image.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Australian-made rocket motor test fired for the first time

children.content: New vision has been released of the first test-firing, a step towards developing a greater Australian-made missile industry.

image.altText: A rocket booster igniting from an engine.

gallery.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

thumbnail.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

items.title: El Niño will 'pour fuel on the fire' as India experiences deadly heatwave

children.content: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

image.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.

items.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine leaves at least 22 dead and dozens injured

children.content: Moscow targeted Kyiv and other cities, killing multiple people, damaging buildings and leaving wounded trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

image.altText: Bright flash and smoke over Kyiv skyline at night.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

items.title: 'Flat-pack' homes hoped to save country towns from 'domino effect'

children.content: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

image.altText: white house without a roof sits on block of land in the outback

items.title: Trump's reportedly 'crazy' phone call to Netanyahu reminds world who calls the shots

children.content: Donald Trump's intervention in Benjamin Netanyahu's plans to strike Beirut goes to the heart of the relationship between the two sides, in that support may be limited, particularly if it threatens what the US president really wants.

image.altText: Donald Trump points his finger towards Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Why Hugh Jackman's brother cried on his first day as a prison-school teacher

children.content: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

gallery.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.
A balding man with cropped hair, wearing a striped button-down shirt.

thumbnail.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.
A balding man with cropped hair, wearing a striped button-down shirt.

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright, and novelist, Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Quiz: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe

children.content: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

image.altText: A statue of a woman with a city in the background and a full moon above her head

items.title: Two men believed trapped in cave for nearly two weeks as rescue mission turns to vertical tunnels

children.content: Rescue efforts for two remaining men believed to be trapped in a cave in central Laos have shifted to investigating deep vertical tunnels in the jungle.

gallery.altText: A man in an orange helmed climbs down a narrow cave, with the only light coming from headlamps.
A diagram of the Laos cave system shows a cavern where two missing men are believed to be.

thumbnail.altText: A man in an orange helmed climbs down a narrow cave, with the only light coming from headlamps.
A diagram of the Laos cave system shows a cavern where two missing men are believed to be.

avatar.alt: Tim Swanston wearing a white shirt stands in front of a camera.

items.title: NSW Police signals body-worn cameras change after Four Corners investigation

children.content: NSW Police will move to the mandatory use of body-worn cameras for sworn officers in the wake of allegations of a culture of impunity within the police force.

image.altText: The chest and arms of two NSW police officers on duty with armed vests on.

items.title: Why the 'dig of the century' is happening underneath Notre Dame

children.content: More than a year after France's famed Notre Dame reopened, an archaeological exploration going thousands of years into the history of Paris has begun just metres below the cathedral's ground floor.

gallery.altText: The Notre Dame cathedral seen on a blue sky day next to a person sheltering under a red umbrella and a dark lightpole.
Workers in white shirts and orange helmets are digging through rock and stone at the bottom of Notre Dame.
A fragment bearing an inscription is photographed among artifacts discovered during excavations outside Notre Dame Cathedral

thumbnail.altText: The Notre Dame cathedral seen on a blue sky day next to a person sheltering under a red umbrella and a dark lightpole.
Workers in white shirts and orange helmets are digging through rock and stone at the bottom of Notre Dame.
A fragment bearing an inscription is photographed among artifacts discovered during excavations outside Notre Dame Cathedral

items.title: Minimum wage rises more than inflation in 'particularly challenging' decision

children.content: Australia's minimum wage will increase by 5.97 per cent, and minimum award workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay boost, in the Fair Work Commission's (FWC) annual wage review.

image.altText: Hands hold a mug under the spout of a coffee machine.

items.title: Plan for Stars and Renegades to merge in BBL shake-up

children.content: Cricket Victoria plans to merge the Stars and Renegades into one 'Melbourne' team.

items.title: Prominent surgeon Dr Reza Adib suspended by health ombudsman

children.content: Obesity surgeon Reza Adib has been suspended from practising medicine as he faces rape charges.

image.altText: A balding man of about 60 in a suit slightly smiles as he walks past reporters' microphones on a suburban street

items.title: 'It is very scary': Parents shocked school was target of alleged deadly plot

children.content: A 13-year-old boy has been charged with violent extremist offences and posed an "imminent threat" to the school.

image.altText: A sign reading st helens state school

items.title: Owning a dog was nothing like I'd imagined

children.content: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

items.title: Government says it preferred second-hand submarines

children.content: Australia and the United States' initial positions about delivering a new submarine under AUKUS differed, Senate estimates has revealed.

children.content: Beer industry stakeholders say it's a "sad day for the history of Tassie beer" after the announcement Boag's brewery in Launceston will close.

items.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer

children.content: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

gallery.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

items.title: Councillor defends calling CEO 'despicable, disgusting woman'

children.content: A regional councillor has justified using offensive language towards the council's CEO in multiple emails and social media videos, claiming that he was attacked first and that "it’s a two-way street".

image.altText: a man leaning against a table with a microphone

items.title: Copper thieves are more brazen and taking exceptional risks, demolition experts say

children.content: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

image.altText: Copper wiring bundles into circles

items.title: One Nation SA says it will not support 'one scrap of legislation' by Labor

children.content: South Australia's One Nation has vowed to block every Labor government bill following a dispute over staffing allocations.

image.altText: composite of Kyam Maher and Cory Bernardi both looking serious
